code:
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of triples into a single fluent sentence.
    Unknown predicates fall back to a generic pattern.
    """
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Simple predicate‑to‑phrase mapping for more natural wording
    # Mapping that returns only the predicate‑phrase (subject will be added later)
    phrase_map = {
        "cityServed": "serves {o}",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevel": "is {o} metres above sea level",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInFeet": "is {o} feet above sea level",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInMetres": "is {o} metres above sea level",
        "location": "is located in {o}",
        "operatingOrganisation": "is operated by {o}",
        "runwayLength": "has a runway length of {o} metres",
        "runwayLengthFeet": "has a runway length of {o} feet",
        "runwayName": "has runway {o}",
        "country": "is in {o}",
        "isPartOf": "is part of {o}",
        "1stRunwayLengthFeet": "has a first runway length of {o} feet",
        "1stRunwayLengthMetre": "has a first runway length of {o} metres",
        "1stRunwayNumber": "has first runway number {o}",
        "1stRunwaySurfaceType": "has a first runway surface of {o}",
        "2ndRunwaySurfaceType": "has a second runway surface of {o}",
        "3rdRunwayLengthFeet": "has a third runway length of {o} feet",
        "3rdRunwaySurfaceType": "has a third runway surface of {o}",
        "4thRunwayLengthFeet": "has a fourth runway length of {o} feet",
        "4thRunwaySurfaceType": "has a fourth runway surface of {o}",
        "5thRunwayNumber": "has fifth runway number {o}",
        "5thRunwaySurfaceType": "has a fifth runway surface of {o}",
        "icaoLocationIdentifier": "has ICAO identifier {o}",
        "locationIdentifier": "has location identifier {o}",
        "iataLocationIdentifier": "has IATA identifier {o}",
        "nativeName": "is also known as {o}",
        "leaderParty": "is governed by the {o} party",
        "capital": "has capital {o}",
        "language": "speaks {o}",
        "leader": "is led by {o}",
        "owner": "is owned by {o}",
        "largestCity": "has largest city {o}",
        "mayor": "has mayor {o}",
        "officialLanguage": "official language is {o}",
        "city": "is a city {o}",
        "jurisdiction": "jurisdiction is {o}",
        "demonym": "residents are called {o}",
        "aircraftHelicopter": "operates helicopter {o}",
        "transportAircraft": "operates transport aircraft {o}",
        "currency": "uses the {o}",
        "headquarter": "has headquarters in {o}",
        "class": "belongs to class {o}",
        "division": "belongs to division {o}",
        "order": "belongs to order {o}",
        "regionServed": "serves the region {o}",
        "leaderTitle": "has leader title {o}",
        "hubAirport": "uses hub airport {o}",
        "aircraftFighter": "flies fighter {o}",
        "attackAircraft": "flies attack aircraft {o}",
        "battle": "participated in {o}",
        "countySeat": "has county seat {o}",
        "chief": "is led by chief {o}",
        "foundedBy": "was founded by {o}",
        "postalCode": "has postal code {o}",
        "areaCode": "has area code {o}",
        "foundingYear": "was founded in {o}",
        "ceremonialCounty": "is in ceremonial county {o}",
    }

    clauses = []
    for t in triples:
        # Strip surrounding quotes from object if present
        obj = t.object
        # Normalize object case for certain predicates
        if t.predicate in ("division", "order", "class"):
            obj = obj.lower()
        if (obj.startswith('"') and obj.endswith('"')) or (obj.startswith("'") and obj.endswith("'")):
            obj = obj[1:-1]
        # Clean language/object suffixes for more natural phrasing
        if t.predicate in ("language", "officialLanguage") and obj.lower().endswith(" language"):
            obj = obj.rsplit(" ", 1)[0]
        # Preserve numeric formatting: keep original decimal part if present,
        # otherwise remove trailing .0 for clean integers.
        try:
            # Detect if original object had a decimal point
            has_decimal = "." in obj
            num = float(obj)
            if num.is_integer():
                if has_decimal:
                    # Keep one decimal place (e.g., 2776.0)
                    obj = f"{int(num)}.0"
                else:
                    obj = str(int(num))
            else:
                # Round to two decimal places, strip trailing zeros
                obj = f"{round(num, 2)}".rstrip('0').rstrip('.')
        except (ValueError, TypeError):
            pass
        tmpl = phrase_map.get(t.predicate)
        if tmpl:
            clause = tmpl.format(s=t.subject, o=obj)
        else:
            # fallback generic wording
            clause = f"{t.subject} has {t.predicate} {obj}"
        clauses.append(clause)

    # Join clauses into a single fluent sentence with proper conjunctions
    if not clauses:
        return ""
    if len(clauses) == 1:
        sentence = clauses[0]
    else:
        sentence = ", ".join(clauses[:-1]) + ", and " + clauses[-1]
    sentence = sentence.rstrip()
    if not sentence.endswith('.'):
        sentence += '.'
    # Capitalize first character
    return sentence[0].upper() + sentence[1:]

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
